cluster: C3 — Logs tell the truth: hold logs -p full dump, -n/--tail = LAST N, delete legacy JSON-log decode shim, jbd2-style sidecar staleness validation, normative HLOGIDX v1 doc + byte-level tests (repo /home/rich/hold/sigmund, branch tempo-refinement, v0.6.1)

play_verdicts.play: kaizen/subtract: delete hold_decode_json_log_line + decoded_log_stream + dead hold_write_json_log_* wrappers (logging.c, core.h, signal.c, filter.c)
play_verdicts.reason: Finding re-verified against source: the decoder (src/core/logging.c:389-412) is called from exactly three read paths (src/viewer/filter.c:204, filter.c:437, src/runtime/signal.c:50) and NO writer emits the wrapper format — hold_write_json_log_entry_fd/bytes_fd (logging.c:381-387) forward to the raw writer and have zero callers; tests/test_hold.sh:874 asserts the wrapper never appears in a raw log, so the decoder can only fire on user data, i.e. it is pure corruption of SPEC.md:50-54 raw-bytes output and hold-on-identity.md:63 script-safe -p. No test anywhere pins decoded output (grep over tests/ finds no {"log" fixture), so nothing pinned breaks. Legacy-log objection dismissed: no normative doc promises pre-0.5 log compatibility, the store layout has changed since, and the owner workstyle forbids legacy shims. Bonus fidelity the play undersells: the tail path's decoded_log_stream_emit uses strlen (signal.c:56), so it also truncates lines at embedded NUL bytes and drops the newline on decoded lines — raw pass-through fixes both.
amendments: The deletion list misses two free(decoded) sites in the backward scanner: filter.c:447 (match branch) and filter.c:476 (else branch) — name them so the mechanical edit does not leave a dangling free or leak; run the ASan lane (scripts/ci.sh) after.

hold_tail_log_until_exit rewrite (signal.c:194-234): replace decoded_log_stream_write with hold_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, buf, (size_t)n) and delete the flush at 228-234; hold_write_all already retries EINTR (src/core/util.c:46) so SIGINT-detach semantics pinned at tests/test_hold.sh:2902-2919 survive.

Regression test must cover BOTH surviving read surfaces: logs -p and the still-live `hold tail` verb (pinned at tests/test_hold.sh:1814, 1843, 1889 and documented as an alias at README.md:126) — grep -F the full {"log":"SNEAKY",...} line byte-identical in both outputs.
Note for the implementer: hold tail may be deleted by the identity cluster later; keep this commit confined to the decoder so it rebases cleanly either way.

play_verdicts.play: lateral/jbd2: validate HLOGIDX header raw_size_bytes against the raw log at open (hold_open_log_index_fd) and load (hold_logidx_map_load); discard stale sidecars
play_verdicts.reason: Finding verified: append_logidx_entry persists raw_size_bytes/raw_mtime_ns on every record (logging.c:340-341) and no reader consults them; hold_open_log_index_fd accepts any parsing header (logging.c:189) and hold_logidx_map_load never compares (242-303), so a copytruncate-rotated log yields non-ascending offsets that violate the binary-search invariant (logging.c:305-318) and fabricated metadata, against filter.c:115's own never-fake rule. Write ordering makes the check race-free on healthy logs: raw bytes land via hold_write_all BEFORE the idx entry and header update (logging.c:353-360), so header.raw_size_bytes > actual size can only mean truncation. The fall-through rebuild path already exists (192-206); the writer-side change is ~4 lines. BUT the play as sketched fails its own goal in the live viewer: tty.c's viewer_reload_idx keeps the OLD in-memory map when reload fails (tty.c:569 `return`), so the stale map keeps rendering fabricated metadata exactly in the rotation scenario the play targets.
amendments: MANDATORY: in src/viewer/tty.c viewer_reload_idx (564-575), on hold_logidx_map_load failure free any loaded map, set idx_loaded=false, and update idx_loaded_raw_size to the observed size — honest no-metadata beats retained stale metadata; without this the play only fixes the plain/open paths.

Keep the check strictly `header.raw_size_bytes > raw size` — equality is the healthy steady state and the redial-append pin (tests/test_hold.sh:3553-3580) plus healthy-sidecar pins (viewer_filter_test.c:357-379, 396-435; chrome OUT/ERR at test_hold.sh:981-984) depend on == being accepted.

Drop mtime from the check entirely and document raw_mtime_ns as advisory: on Linux/glibc `#elif defined(st_mtim)` (logging.c:37) is false (st_mtim is a struct field, not a macro), so the stored value is second-granularity — a regression comparison would be noise.

Reader side: keep the (log_path, out) signature; lstat(log_path) inside hold_logidx_map_load, reject non-regular or missing raw log along with staleness (errno EINVAL/ESTALE) — no API change, the two existing test call sites stay untouched.

Do NOT duplicate play 3's recovery tests here; this play adds exactly two new C cases: reader rejects stale (truncate raw below header high-water → load returns -1) and writer rebuilds on reopen (fresh header, entry_count 0).

Document the residual misses honestly in the store.md section (play 4): size high-water is a heuristic — copytruncate followed by regrowth past the old size, and rename-rotation against a viewer's already-open fd, escape it; a generation tag is v2 material (deferral D3), not more machinery now.

play_verdicts.play: tests/auditor: independent-decoder byte-layout + crash-recovery cases in viewer_filter_test.c (torn entry, header overcount, 1MiB TRUNCATED clamp, v1 byte pin)
play_verdicts.reason: All layout assumptions re-verified against write_logidx_header (logging.c:94-110): magic@0, version u16@8, header_size u16@10, entry_size u16@12, flags u32@16 (bit0 = little-endian), base_unix_us u64@24, entry_count u64@32, raw_size_bytes u64@40, raw_mtime_ns u64@48; entries pack offset bits 0-43 and len-1 bits 44-63 in the first u64, delta-us bits 0-47 and meta bits 48-63 in the second (logging.c:332-333); meta bits confirmed at logging.c:13-16. Arithmetic in the sketch checks out: idx truncated to 80+2*16+7 gives physical=(39)/16=2 < header 3 → count 2; pwrite LE 9 at offset 32 → min(9,2)=2; a (1<<20)+16 newline-free write clamps len to 1<<20 and sets TRUNCATED|NO_NEWLINE (logging.c:327-330, 374). build_indexed_log (viewer_filter_test.c:325-348) only ever makes healthy sidecars, so the durability rules SPEC.md:50-53 leans on are indeed unpinned. make test already runs viewer_filter_test (Makefile:55-64) and the macOS lane builds it (.github/scripts/test_macos_ci.sh:99). Cheapest play in the cluster and it must land FIRST so plays 1/2/5 diff against pinned rules.
amendments: Sequence: commit 1 of the cluster, before any behavior change.

The byte-layout case must encode the len-1 trap explicitly (on-disk field 7 for an 8-byte record) and carry the play's own required comment: local #defines are deliberate double-entry — do not 'fix' by including logging.c's private constants.
Extend the layout case with the header cheap-checks while there: magic bytes, version==1@8, header_size==80@10, entry_size==16@12, flags bit0@16, entry_count==2@32, ascending offsets.

TRUNCATED case: heap-allocate the 1MiB+16 buffer (stack would blow) and also assert NO_NEWLINE (0x0002) is set alongside TRUNCATED (0x0008) — that pair is exactly what the wrong future-doc layout would mis-decode as STDIN.
Own ALL recovery/byte tests for the cluster — plays 2 and 4 must reference these, not re-add near-duplicates (play 2's sketch case (b)/(c) and play 4's recovery cases are this play's cases).

play_verdicts.play: first-principles/warden: rewrite docs/store.md with normative HLOGIDX v1 byte layout; stamp future-doc layout superseded; archive launcher.md/console.md; add python3 shell byte test
play_verdicts.reason: Core claim verified: SPEC.md:50-53 and hold-on-identity.md:29-31 promise a documented stable format; docs/store.md never mentions the sidecar and is badly stale (documents profiles.json/aliases.json, requires_elevation, `hold tail <id>` as primary surface, and links to quickstart.md/profiles-and-aliases.md/security.md which do not exist in docs/); the only written byte spec (docs/future/viewer-fixes-before-0.5.md:413-424: 3-bit stream field, NO_NEWLINE=0x0008) contradicts the shipped writer (logging.c:13-16), so a third-party reader following the doc decodes every partial stdout line as STDIN. The store.md rewrite + supersede stamp is squarely this cluster. But the play is oversized for one commit and triple-books the layout: shipped code + play 3's C decoder + a new python3 shell test + the doc is four copies of the same bytes, and the launcher.md/console.md archive sweep plus index.md/SPEC.md link surgery is a different drift concern that collides with other clusters' likely doc touches.
amendments: KEEP: docs/store.md rewritten for 0.6 (records+logs under the user base, runs/logs/console/public under the system base, no profiles/aliases/elevation, dead links removed) plus a normative 'HLOGIDX v1' section transcribed from logging.c:4-19/94-110/320-343 — including the len-1 encoding, entry-then-header commit order, min(header,physical) recovery, 1MiB clamp→TRUNCATED, the play-2 size-tie staleness rule, raw_mtime_ns marked advisory (seconds granularity on Linux), and an unknown-version rule (readers reject version != 1) so v2 stays possible.
KEEP: supersede stamp on docs/future/viewer-fixes-before-0.5.md's layout block ('shipped v1 differs: single-bit stream flags').

CUT → deferral D1 'docs-archive-sweep': git mv launcher.md/console.md to docs/archive/ and the index.md:27-33 / SPEC.md:8-9 reference restructure — real drift, separate concern, separate commit outside this cluster.

CUT → deferral D2: the python3 shell byte test — rejected as a third booking of the layout; play 3's independent C decoder is the executable spec, and test_hold.sh:872-873 already smoke-checks magic end-to-end through the real binary.
Land LAST (commit 5) so the doc can state the staleness rule that actually shipped in commit 4.

play_verdicts.play: UNPLAYED CLUSTER MANDATE: logs -p full dump + -n/--tail = last N (findings src/viewer/filter.c:6 critical and src/runtime/signal.c:771 high — no dossier play implements them)
play_verdicts.reason: The cluster's headline critical has no play in the dossier; the Skeptic flags the gap and specifies it. Findings re-verified: hold_log_filter_options_init defaults visible_capacity and max_results to 50 (filter.c:6, 29-31); the non-follow plain path runs one forward hold_log_filter_fd from offset 0 (signal.c:892-899) stopping at 50 (filter.c:139, 224) — so -p silently prints the FIRST 50; -n/--tail/--limit alias onto max_results (signal.c:771-782) making -n N the FIRST N, inverting Docker and README.md:127 which already promises 'last 100 records'. Absurdity confirmed at the -f boundary: plain -f on an EXITED call already dumps the whole log in 50-line batches (stream_view_follow_until_exit loops on next_offset, signal.c:533-547), so -f prints more than -p today. hold_log_filter_backward_fd already implements newest-N (filter.c:333). No existing test pins first-N with more matches than N: every --limit/-n use has a single matching line or a <50-line log (test_hold.sh:876, 888-895, 2582-2588, 3235, 3515-3516; test_040_dockerish.sh:53-63) — the change lands without touching a pinned shape, which is itself the indictment.
amendments: Full dump: when no limit flag was given, run a forward batch loop in the non-follow plain branch (repeat hold_log_filter_fd, lseek to result.next_offset, print each batch, accumulate stats, stop when line_count==0 — a batch either fills max_results or reaches EOF, so it terminates, including on a still-running call, which .github/scripts/test_macos_ci.sh:38-41 depends on). Emit ONE aggregated debug-stats line to preserve the stderr shape pinned at test_hold.sh:894-895.

Last-N: track `bool have_limit` in the flag loop; when set, anchor hold_log_filter_backward_fd at EOF. The remedy hint's 'uncapped byte budget' is a trap: byte_budget==0 maps to 1MiB (filter.c:347) and the engine mallocs the whole span (filter.c:378) — so implement doubling rescans in the CALLER (1MiB, x4, until line_count==max_results or the window reaches offset 0), leaving engine semantics alone (viewer_filter_test.c:301-321 pins the short-budget behavior).

-f composition: with -n N, run the backward last-N preamble, print it, then seek to the preamble's next_offset and enter the follow loop (skip its from_end lseek) — Docker's --tail-with-follow; on an exited call this prints last N and exits. Without -n, leave -f exactly as is (running: EOF-start per the design comment at signal.c:858-860; exited: full dump) — changing -f history semantics is deferral D5, owner redline required.
parse_view_limit's 1..10000 clamp (signal.c:448-456) stays; -n larger than the log returns the whole log.
Interactive viewer untouched: tty.c:551-553 overrides capacity with viewer_body_rows — the full-screen 50-ish visible window is design, not a bug, per the cluster charter.

pinned_conflicts.what: Pins that no writer ever emits the {"log":...} wrapper into a raw log — this is the evidence making the decoder decode-only dead weight; must stay untouched and green through the shim deletion.

__view --filter/--similar --limit 1 shapes plus the stderr debug-stats line 'bytes_read=... visible=1'; these now ride the new backward last-N path — each has exactly one matching line so output is unchanged, but the implementation MUST still emit print_view_result's stats line on the backward path or 895 fails.
logs --plain -n 50 on logs shorter than 50 lines — compatible with last-N only because the logs are short; rerun the root lane after the semantics flip.

Polls `logs -n 1 --plain` for '^ok$' on a single-line log; compatible with last-N, but it is the test that SHOULD have caught the inversion — the new last-N test supersedes its coverage, do not modify it.
logs --plain/-p exact-line greps and the redial-append count '^named$' == 2; full-dump default keeps both green (2-line log); confirm in the dockerish lane.

hold tail content and Ctrl-C-detach rc==0 pins; the raw pass-through rewrite of hold_tail_log_until_exit (signal.c:194-234) must preserve content and SIGINT semantics (hold_write_all retries EINTR, util.c:46).
Pins that hold_log_filter_backward_fd with a short byte budget returns 0 lines + scan_limited; the last-N feature must grow the budget from the caller, never 'fix' the engine, or this pin breaks.

pinned_conflicts.where: tests/viewer_filter_test.c:357-379 and 396-435; tests/test_hold.sh:975-985 (chrome test, 'src:all', 'OUT | chrome one', 'ERR | chrome err'); tests/test_hold.sh:3553-3580 (redial append)
pinned_conflicts.what: Healthy-sidecar loads and viewer metadata rendering; play 2's staleness check must be strictly header.raw_size_bytes > raw size (equality = healthy steady state, including restart-append) or all of these break.
logs -p on a symlinked log must still exit nonzero with 'failed to open log'; the -p rework must not move filtering ahead of the O_NOFOLLOW open at signal.c:845.
logs -p on a still-RUNNING call must terminate promptly with captured output; the full-dump batch loop must exit at EOF (line_count==0), never poll like follow.

implementation_risks: 1. Memory blowup in last-N: hold_log_filter_backward_fd mallocs the entire scanned span (filter.c:378) and byte_budget==0 silently means 1MiB, not unlimited (filter.c:347) — the finding's 'uncapped byte budget' remedy is unimplementable as written. Use caller-side doubling rescans (1MiB, x4, stop at enough matches or offset 0); worst case (single multi-GB line) is no worse than the existing forward line-realloc path, but say so in a comment.

2. Stale in-memory map in the viewer: tty.c:568-571 returns on map-load failure and KEEPS the old map — without the amendment, play 2's staleness rejection never reaches the live full-screen viewer, the highest-value surface for the fix.

3. Full-dump loop correctness: must terminate on running calls (batch either fills max_results or hits EOF), must aggregate stats into one debug-stats stderr line, and must handle the trailing partial line exactly once (hold_log_filter_fd's EOF branch, filter.c:273-287, already consumes it; the loop then sees line_count==0 and stops).

4. -f/-n composition: define it (backward last-N preamble, then follow from preamble next_offset) or -n silently degrades to a batch size in follow mode after the semantics flip — an incoherence worse than today's.

5. Free-discipline in the decode removal: six free(decoded) sites (filter.c:210, 214, 220, 223, 447, 476) plus the strdup-per-line hot-path removal; run the ASan lane in scripts/ci.sh before and after commit 2.

6. Cross-cluster collision: src/runtime/signal.c and src/console/broker.c are active surfaces for the console/endings cluster (recent repo memory shows broker/exit-status work); keep commits 2 and 3 confined to hold_tail_log_until_exit, hold_cmd_view_action, and the stream helpers, and rebase before landing.

7. Staleness heuristic false negatives: copytruncate followed by regrowth past the old high-water, and rename-rotation against a viewer's already-open fd, escape the size check — document as heuristic in store.md; a generation tag is v2 (deferral D3), do not balloon now.

8. Linux mtime is seconds-granularity: logging.c:37's #elif defined(st_mtim) is false under glibc (st_mtim is a field, not a macro), so raw_mtime_ns is st_mtime*1e9 — any mtime-based staleness comparison would be a no-op or misfire; omit it and mark the field advisory in the doc.

9. Doc freeze: the v1 single STDERR bit cannot express STDIN/PTY (console captures are written as "stdout", broker.c:408) — the normative doc must carry the unknown-version reader rule so the eventual stream-labeling fix can rev to v2 instead of mutating v1.

10. Test-lane cost: the 1MiB TRUNCATED case runs under ASan on both CI lanes — heap-allocate the buffer, one write, keep runtime negligible; macOS lane builds viewer_filter_test explicitly (.github/scripts/test_macos_ci.sh:99), so new cases must stay POSIX-portable (pwrite/ftruncate only).
revised_change_set:
Five commits, in order; make + make test + scripts/lint_layers.sh green after each; no legacy shims, no new deps, C11 static binary unchanged.

COMMIT 1 — pin the sidecar before touching it (tests only). File: tests/viewer_filter_test.c. Add four cases reusing build_indexed_log and register in main(): (a) test_logidx_recovers_torn_entry — append a third line, ftruncate the .idx to 80+2*16+7, hold_logidx_map_load returns count==2 with both survivors intact; (b) test_logidx_recovers_header_overcount — pwrite u64 LE 9 at idx offset 32, reload count==2; (c) test_logidx_truncated_meta_bit — one heap-allocated (1<<20)+16-byte newline-free hold_write_indexed_log_bytes_fd write, record len==1<<20 and meta has TRUNCATED (0x0008) and NO_NEWLINE (0x0002); (d) test_logidx_v1_byte_layout — locally #defined constants (comment: deliberate double-entry, do not include logging.c's defines), assert header magic/version 1@8/header_size 80@10/entry_size 16@12/flags bit0@16/entry_count 2@32, entry u64s: offset bits 0-43, len-1 bits 44-63 (field 7 for the 8-byte record), meta bits 48-63 with stderr entry carrying exactly 0x0001, offsets ascending.

COMMIT 2 — delete the JSON decode shim. Files: src/core/logging.c (delete lines 381-412: both dead wrappers + hold_decode_json_log_line), include/hold/core.h (delete decls 119-121), src/runtime/signal.c (delete struct decoded_log_stream and its four helpers, lines 29-87; in hold_tail_log_until_exit replace the decoder write at 197 with hold_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, buf, (size_t)n) and drop the flush block at 228-234), src/viewer/filter.c (consume_line: remove decoded/visible detour at 201-207 and the four free(decoded) at 210/214/220/223, pass line/n straight through; backward scanner: remove 434-440 and free(decoded) at 447 and 476, dup_line the raw slice). New shell test test_logs_json_looking_line_roundtrips in tests/test_hold.sh near test_log_capture: launch -d /bin/sh -c with a literal {"log":"SNEAKY","stream":"stdout"} echo; grep -F the byte-identical line in both `hold logs -p` and `hold tail` output; register with run_test near line 4134. Verify with ASan lane.

COMMIT 3 — plain output tells the truth (the unplayed cluster mandate). File: src/runtime/signal.c. In hold_cmd_view_action's flag loop (771-782) set bool have_limit. Non-follow plain branch (892-899): if !have_limit, run a forward batch loop — hold_log_filter_fd, print_view_result(per batch, stats deferred), lseek to result.next_offset, repeat until line_count==0; aggregate bytes_read/lines_scanned/match_count/line_count and emit one debug-stats line. If have_limit, run hold_log_filter_backward_fd anchored at EOF with caller-side doubling byte budget (1MiB, x4, until line_count==max_results or window start==0), then print_view_result(result, debug_stats). Follow branch: if have_limit, run the same backward preamble, print it, lseek to its next_offset, then enter stream_view_follow_until_exit with from_end=false so the preamble seam has no gap or duplication; without -n, follow is byte-for-byte unchanged. New tests in tests/test_hold.sh: test_logs_plain_dumps_entire_log (120-line seq call; logs -p line count 120, first line 1, last line 120), test_logs_tail_flag_returns_newest (-n 3 -> exactly 118/119/120; --tail=1 -> 120; -n 10000 -> all 120), test_logs_follow_tail_on_exited_call (-p -f -n 2 -> exactly the last 2 lines, rc 0). README.md:127 already promises last-N; no doc edit required.

COMMIT 4 — jbd2 staleness validation. Files: src/core/logging.c — in hold_open_log_index_fd after read_logidx_header succeeds (189), if h.raw_size_bytes > (uint64_t)st.st_size (st already fstat'ed from raw_log_fd at 176) fall through to the existing ftruncate+fresh-header path (192-206); in hold_logidx_map_load after the header read (260-264), lstat(log_path) and reject (close, errno, return -1) when the raw log is missing/non-regular or h.raw_size_bytes exceeds its size — strictly greater-than, no mtime check. src/viewer/tty.c — viewer_reload_idx (564-575): on load failure free any loaded map, idx_loaded=false, idx_loaded_raw_size=size. Tests in viewer_filter_test.c: test_logidx_map_load_rejects_stale_sidecar (truncate raw below the header high-water, load returns -1) and test_open_log_index_rebuilds_on_stale (reopen via hold_open_log_index_fd after raw shrink yields entry_count 0 and a subsequent append indexes at the new offset).

COMMIT 5 — the format becomes a written contract. Files: docs/store.md (full rewrite for 0.6: user base holds <64hex>.json records and <64hex>.log/.log.idx pairs plus console/; system base runs/logs/console/public; delete profiles/aliases/requires_elevation/hold-tail prose and the dead quickstart/profiles-and-aliases/security links; add normative 'HLOGIDX v1' section transcribed from src/core/logging.c:4-19, 94-110, 320-343 — 80-byte LE header with field offsets, 16-byte entries with the len-1 encoding, meta bits STDERR=0x0001/NO_NEWLINE=0x0002/CONTINUATION=0x0004/TRUNCATED=0x0008, ascending-offset invariant, entry-then-header commit order, min(header,physical) recovery rule, 1MiB clamp -> TRUNCATED, the size-tie staleness rule from commit 4, raw_mtime_ns advisory, readers must reject version != 1); docs/future/viewer-fixes-before-0.5.md — stamp the layout block (~395-435) 'superseded by docs/store.md: shipped v1 uses single-bit stream flags'. Grep-check doc links.

NAMED DEFERRALS (out of this cluster): D1 docs-archive-sweep (git mv launcher.md/console.md to docs/archive/, index.md:20-40 and SPEC.md:8-9 restructure); D2 python3 shell-level byte test (triple-booking; the C decoder is the executable spec); D3 sidecar generation tag for rotation cases the size heuristic misses (v2); D4 plain -t/--time/--date from the sidecar (identity.md:64 promise, findings signal.c:758 / hold-on-identity.md:64 — separate contract decision); D5 -f history semantics without -n (Docker prints history then follows; Hold seeks EOF per the design comment at signal.c:858-860 — owner redline); D6 embedded-NUL truncation in print_view_result (signal.c:459-469 strlen) — needs byte-length plumbing through hold_log_filter_result, separate small fix.
test_plan: NEW (shell, test_hold.sh idiom — isolated store via the harness HOME, run_test registration in the list at ~4106-4160, poll-don't-sleep like test_docker_shaped_cli_flags_and_rm:3231-3240): test_logs_plain_dumps_entire_log — id=$("$HOLD_BIN" -d -- /bin/sh -c 'seq 1 120' | extract_id); poll until [ "$("$HOLD_BIN" logs -p "$id" | wc -l)" -eq 120 ]; assert head -n1 == 1 and tail -n1 == 120. Register: run_test "hold logs -p dumps the whole raw log".

NEW (shell): test_logs_tail_flag_returns_newest — same 120-line call; "$HOLD_BIN" logs -p -n 3 "$id" emits exactly 118,119,120 (compare against printf fixture); --tail=1 emits 120 only; -n 10000 emits all 120. Register: run_test "logs -n and --tail select the newest records".

NEW (shell): test_logs_json_looking_line_roundtrips — launch -d /bin/sh -c 'echo '"'"'{"log":"SNEAKY","stream":"stdout"}'"'"''; assert grep -F of the full literal line in `logs -p` output AND in `hold tail` output (both read paths post-shim). Register near run_test "logging captures stdout+stderr" (4134).
NEW (shell): test_logs_follow_tail_on_exited_call — 5-line exited call; "$HOLD_BIN" logs -p -f -n 2 "$id" returns rc 0 and exactly the last two lines (backward preamble + immediate exit at EOF).

NEW (C, tests/viewer_filter_test.c, registered in main()): test_logidx_recovers_torn_entry (ftruncate idx to 80+2*16+7 -> count==2, survivors intact); test_logidx_recovers_header_overcount (pwrite u64 LE 9 at offset 32 -> count==2); test_logidx_truncated_meta_bit ((1<<20)+16 newline-free write -> len==1<<20, meta & 0x0008 and & 0x0002); test_logidx_v1_byte_layout (local constants + deliberate-double-entry comment; header fields at documented offsets, len-1 encoding, stderr meta 0x0001 in bits 48-63, ascending offsets).

NEW (C, commit 4): test_logidx_map_load_rejects_stale_sidecar (truncate raw log below header raw_size_bytes -> hold_logidx_map_load returns -1); test_open_log_index_rebuilds_on_stale (hold_open_log_index_fd on shrunken raw -> fresh header, entry_count 0, next append indexed at the new offset).
